chief_complaint: A 63-year-old patient presents with atrial fibrillation, with a predominance of women.
history: The patient has a history of high blood pressure, with heart failure being the most frequent circumstance of discovery. The patient's atrial fibrillation is persistent in 52.24% of cases and valvular AF accounts for 31%.
exam: Left ventricular systolic function was impaired in 55.7%, and the left atrium was dilated in 70.83%.
labs: ECG confirmed atrial fibrillation, with 24.4% of patients having high blood pressure. CHA2DS2VASC score was >/= 2 in patients who received anticoagulation with LMWH and oral VKA.
